expectations.evidence: Describing defects with reproduction steps is general QA practice. Naive Claude would describe what actions led to the defect.
Expected vs actual is a standard defect report format. Naive Claude would include this naturally when documenting a test failure.

Without the skill's severity framework (coverage gate failures vs browser functional failures, Dutch gov compliance levels), naive Claude would assign ad-hoc severity labels (high/medium/low) without a defined scale tied to Conduction's quality requirements.
Without knowing plan.json's task structure, naive Claude would not know which task number to link a defect to. It might reference the feature area but not the specific task ID from plan.json.

notes: Reproduction steps and expected/actual are general QA practice. Structured severity (tied to coverage gates and compliance levels) and plan.json task linkage require skill knowledge.
